mechanism_narrative: Monthly growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumption expenditures, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or top-quartile PAYEMS growth months do not show significantly higher 20-day returns than bottom-quartile months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5%, restrictive monetary policy raises short-term borrowing costs above trend nominal growth, compressing corporate profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC is greater than 0 during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated CPI inflation and low unemployment indicate an overheating economy that forces the Federal Reserve to accelerate tightening, repricing equity discount rates downward and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of CPIAUCSL and UNRATE achieves Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or the joint signal underperforms the univariate CPIAUCSL signal.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery, accelerating industrial production growth signals reviving durable goods demand and manufacturing operating leverage, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of months during post-recession recovery when INDPRO is accelerating, or Rank IC drops below 0.03 over the recovery period.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand that precedes downward earnings revisions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered months, or Rank IC is positive over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts combined with rising unemployment predicts concurrent collapse in interest-rate-sensitive durable goods demand and labor income, generating strongly negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year window, or the HOUST and UNRATE combination fails to outperform either series alone in predicting returns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth falling below zero signals manufacturing recession and inventory destocking that reduces aggregate corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered months, or Rank IC is positive over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the unemployment rate reduce aggregate labor income and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days as the negative macro signal is rapidly priced into equities.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or UNRATE changes show a positive correlation with 5-day forward returns.
